chunks.page_summary: This page describes Rheonics' "Fixed Transmitter" (FT) setup, which integrates the electronics directly into SRV viscometers and SRD density meters, and provides instructions on how to request and configure this option.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Inline Process Density Meter SRD and viscometer SRV with integrated display transmitter

Modified on: Tue, 3 Dec, 2024 at 6:36 PM

What is the purpose of this article?
Show Rheonics SRV viscometer and SRD density and viscosity meter with SME electronics (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/) integrated in a Fixed Transmit

What products are involved?
(https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) and (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/)
SRV - Inline Viscosity Meter
SRD - Inline Density and Viscosity meter

Rheonics Sensor "Fixed Transmitter" Setup
Rheonics SRV viscometer and SRD density and viscosity meter comprise the sensor probe, the sensor cable, and the electronics. All three parts are required for the sensor to operate. The sensor probe is installed in line and in contact with the fluid, while the electronics are usually placed elsewhere in an electronic cabinet (SME-DRM) or outdoors and fixed on a near wall or operator’s accessible area (SME-TRD and -TR), with the sensor cable running from the probe to the electronics.

Rheonics offers a "Fixed Transmitter" (–FT) setup that allows the SRV Inline Viscometer or SRD Density and Viscosity meter to have the electronics SME unit integrated directly, creating a single-piece sensor unit installed in the line containing all features of communication and local display.

This setup is compatible with SME-TRD (includes display) and -TR (blind cover) electronics variants. Review all electronic variants here (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/). Review SME CAD models here (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411379-rheonics-sensor-electronics-sme-drawings-and-3d-models).

Figure 1: Rheonics FT Setup: SRV and SRD with SME-TRD integrated transmitter

When Rheonics FT Setup is ordered by client, the sensor is delivered fully assembled for installation. If reassembly is required on site, contact Rheonics Team for instructions.

How to request and configure?

The "FT" Setup is a sensor configuration feature that is added to the full sensor configuration codes.

To order a Rheonics sensor with integrated transmitter, the "FT" Fixed Transmitter option should be selected in the online SRV RFQ (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srv/) or SRD RFQ (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srd/). Electronics variants SME-TRD (E1) and -TR (E2) are compatible.

Electronics (select one) *
Please refer to the Electronics and Communications Page for more information.
E1 : SME-TRD : Transmitter housing with display
E2 : SME-TR : Transmitter housing with solid cover
E3 : SME-DRM : DIN-rail mount housing
FT Option - Fixed Transmitter Sensor
Figure 2: FT Option in RFQ Electronics Section

Next, an order code structure shows the relevant codes for the FT setup.
How to Order Rheonics Fixed Transmitter Sensor?
key_facts: Rheonics SRV viscometers and SRD density and viscosity meters typically consist of a sensor probe, sensor cable, and separate electronics (SME-DRM, SME-TRD, -TR).
The "Fixed Transmitter" (–FT) setup integrates the electronics SME unit directly into the SRV or SRD sensor.
The FT setup creates a single-piece sensor unit installed in the line, providing all communication and local display features.
The FT setup is compatible with SME-TRD (includes display) and -TR (blind cover) electronics variants.
When the Rheonics FT Setup is ordered, the sensor is delivered fully assembled for installation.
The "FT" Setup is a sensor configuration feature added to the full sensor configuration codes during ordering.

E1 : SME-TRD : Transmitter housing with display
E2 : SME-TR : Transmitter housing with solid cover
Figure 2: FT Option in RFQ Electronics Section

Please refer to the Electronics and Communications Page for more information.

chunks.page_summary: This page outlines the Rheonics SR-type sensor product code form, specifying options for sensor type, electronics, transmitter setup, and probe variants, along with references for further configuration details.
chunks.extracted_text:
Code Form: SR_ - E_ - FT - X_
Example: SRV - E1 - FT - X1-34N

1 Rheonics SR- Type sensor
  SRV: Inline Viscometer
  SRD: Inline Density and Viscosity meter

2 Electronics Variant
  E1: SME-TRD electronics in housing with display
  E2: SME-TR electronics in blind housing

3 FT - Fixed Transmitter Setup
  Fixed Transmitter on Probe Sensor Setup

4 Sensor Probe Variant
  Visit SRV variants (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000297658/) and SRD variants (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000297659/)

Additional information and codes are required for the complete sensor configuration in online SRV RFQ (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000297658/) and SRD RFQ (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srd/).

References
[1]: Rheonics SME Electronics variants (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/)
key_facts: The product code for Rheonics SR-type sensors is composed of four main segments: sensor type, electronics variant, fixed transmitter setup, and sensor probe variant.
SRD indicates an Inline Density and Viscosity meter.
The E1 electronics variant includes SME-TRD electronics with a display in its housing.
The E2 electronics variant includes SME-TR electronics in a blind housing.
FT stands for Fixed Transmitter Setup, signifying that the transmitter is integrated into the probe sensor setup.
Further details and codes for complete sensor configuration are available through online Request for Quotation (RFQ) processes for SRV and SRD sensors.
Specific variant details for SRV and SRD sensors are accessible via provided support links.
Information regarding Rheonics SME Electronics variants is available through a dedicated URL in the references section.
